ops.title: ship CI-watch discovery gaps: analysis + hardening in 2411bea2
ops.body: 2026-07-14 investigation (claude-pool red-lint episode): ship's failure REPORTING was always correct — red runs produce a 'CI failure' segment, named failing jobs, a log excerpt, and exit 1 (the episode was an orchestration-policy gap, since closed by the fleet fix-forward rule in cc-skills version-control.md). The latent gaps were all in run DISCOVERY, fixed in 2411bea2: (1) zero runs registered in the 60s poll window returned benign 'CI no-run'/exit 0 even when the repo has workflows — now 'CI unconfirmed' + exit 1 when .github/workflows contains yml/yaml (workflow-less repos keep the benign path); (2) the settle loop broke on the first re-list that added nothing, racing workflows that register seconds later — now a two-poll quiet horizon (~10s, resets whenever a new run appears); (3) gh run list --limit 10 hard-capped discovery — now 50; (4) a head-SHA failure after a successful push suppressed the committed/pushed confirmation — now printed before the error. Residual edges, deliberate: the workflow probe uses os.Getwd(), so from a repo subdirectory the unconfirmed case degrades to benign no-run; repos whose workflows are paths-filtered or dispatch-only now exit 1 on legitimately run-less pushes (the error names this and gives 'gh run list --commit <sha>'); every ship settles ~10s after the last run concludes (latency cost of the race fix).
